options.name: --help
options.description: Shows this message and exits

options.name: --baseappdatapath
options.description: Set the base config and appData path

options.name: --hostname
options.description:
Specifies the server hostname. Used in auto-
generated TLS certificate for SMTP if enabled.

options.name: --allowremoteconnections
options.description:
Specifies if remote connections will be allowed to
the SMTP and IMAP servers. Use -
allowremoteconnections+ to enable or -
allowremoteconnections- to disable

options.name: --bindaddress
options.description:
Specifies the IP address to bind to for SMTP and
IMAP servers. If not specified, the behavior is
determined by allowremoteconnections

options.name: --disableipv6
options.description:
If true, SMTP and IMAP servers will NOT listen
using IPv6 Dual Stack

options.name: --smtpport
options.description:
Set the port the SMTP server listens on. Specify 0
to assign automatically

options.name: --db
options.description:
Specifies the path where the database will be
stored relative to APPDATA env var on Windows or
XDG_CONFIG_HOME on non-Windows. Specify "" to
use an in memory database.

options.name: --messagestokeep
options.description: mailbox

options.name: --tlsmode
options.description:
Specifies the TLS mode to use for SMTP only. (POP3
uses --pop3tlsmode). Valid options: None,
StartTls, ImplicitTls.

options.name: --tlscertificatestorethumbprint
options.description:
Specifies the thumbprint to find the certificate
from the computer's store to use for SMTP if TLS
is enabled/requested. This must be an X509.
Specify "" to use the path option, or an auto-
generated self-signed certificate (then see
console output on first startup).

options.name: --tlscertificate
options.description:
if TLS is enabled/requested. This must be an
X509 certificate - generally a .CER, .CRT or .
PFX file. If using .CER or .CRT, you must
provide the private key separately using --
tlscertificateprivatekey.  Specify "" to use an
auto-generated self-signed certificate (then see

options.name: --tlscertificateprivatekey
options.description:
Specifies the corresponding private key file for
the SMTP TLS certificate if the private key is
not part of the TlsCertificate file

options.name: --tlscertificatepassword
options.description:
Specifies the password for  SMTP TLSCertificate/
TlsCertificatePrivateKey

options.name: --relaysmtpserver
options.description:
Sets the name of the SMTP server that will be used
to relay messages or "" if messages relay should
not be allowed

options.name: --relaysmtpport
options.description:
Sets the port number for the SMTP server used to
relay messages

options.name: --relayautomaticallyemails
options.description:
A comma separated list of recipient addresses for
which messages will be relayed automatically. An
empty list means that no messages are relayed

options.name: --relaysenderaddress
options.description:
Specifies the address used in MAIL FROM when
relaying messages. (Sender address in message
headers is left unmodified). The sender of each
message is used if not specified.

options.name: --relayusername
options.description:
The username for the SMTP server used to relay
messages. If "" no authentication is attempted

options.name: --relaypassword
options.description:
The password for the SMTP server used to relay
messages

options.name: --relaytlsmode
options.description:
Sets the TLS mode when connecting to relay SMTP
server. See: http://www.mimekit.net/docs/html/T_
MailKit_Security_SecureSocketOptions.htm

options.name: --imapport
options.description:
Specifies the port the IMAP server will listen on -
allows standard email clients to view/retrieve
messages. Specify --imapport="" to disable the
IMAP server.

options.name: --pop3port
options.description:
Specifies the port the POP3 server will listen on -
allows standard email clients to retrieve
messages. Specify --pop3port="" to disable the
POP3 server.

options.name: --pop3tlsmode
options.description:
Specifies the TLS mode for POP3 (None|StartTls|
ImplicitTls).

options.name: --pop3secureconnectionrequired
options.description: Require secure connection (TLS) for POP3 clients

options.name: --nousersettings
options.description: Skip loading of appsetttings.json file in %APPDATA%

options.name: --debugsettings
options.description: Prints out most settings values on startup

options.name: --recreatedb
options.description: Recreates the DB on startup if it already exists

options.name: --locksettings
options.description:
Locks settings from being changed by user via web
interface

options.name: --installpath
options.description:
Sets path to folder containing wwwroot and other
files

options.name: --disablemessagesanitisation
options.description: Disables message HTML sanitisation.

options.name: --authenticationrequired
options.description: Requires that SMTP and IMAP clients authenticate

options.name: --secureconnectionrequired
options.description: Requires that SMTP clients use SSL/TLS

options.name: --smtpallowanycredentials
options.description:
True if the SMTP server will allow any credentials
to be used without checking them again the '
Users'

options.name: --webauthenticationrequired
options.description: Require authentication for web interface

options.name: --delivermessagestousersdefaultmailbox
options.description:
True if the mailbox recipient filter should be
ignored for authenticated smtp sessions and
messages should be delivered to the user's
default mailbox instead

options.name: --user
options.description:
Adds a user and password combination for web, SMTP
and IMAP. Use format username=password. This
option can be repeated to add multiple users.

options.name: --SmtpAuthTypesNotSecure
options.description:
SMTP auth type enabled when not using secure
connection (choices: ANONYMOUS, PLAIN, LOGIN,
CRAM-MD5). Separate values with comma.

options.name: --SmtpAuthTypesSecure
options.description: SMTP auth type enabled when  using secure

options.name: --mailbox
options.description:
Adds a mailbox in Name=Recipients format or as
JSON. See appsettings.json for JSON format with
header/source filters. This option can be
repeated to add multiple mailboxes.

options.name: --sslprotocols
options.description:
Specifies the SSL/TLS protocol version(s) that
will be allowed. Separate with commas. See https:
//learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.
security.authentication.sslprotocols?view=net-9.0

options.name: --tlsciphersuites
options.description:
Specifies the TLS cipher suites to be allowed. Not
supported on Windows. Separate with commas. See
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/
system.net.security.tlsciphersuite?view=net-9.0

options.name: --HtmlValidateConfigfile
options.description:
Defines path to a config file used for HTML
validation. See https://html-validate.org/usage/
index.html#configuration

options.name: --maxmessagesize
options.description: by the SMTP server

options.name: --tui
options.description:
Run with Terminal User Interface (TUI) instead of
web interface

options.name: --delivertostdout
options.description:
Specifies mailboxes (comma-separated) or '*' to
output received raw message content to stdout

options.name: --exitafter
options.description:
Specifies the number of messages to receive before
delivertostdout)

options.name: --oauth2authority
options.description:
OAuth2/XOAUTH2 Identity Provider authority URL for
token validation (e.g., https://login.
microsoftonline.com/common/v2.0)

options.name: --oauth2audience
options.description: validation

options.name: --oauth2issuer
options.description:
OAuth2/XOAUTH2 expected issuer value for token
validation (optional, defaults to authority's
issuer)

options.name: --service
options.description:
when running as a Windows service (
started by the Service Control Manager). To
install the service use --install-service.

options.name: --install-service
options.description:
Installs smtp4dev as a Windows service and exits.
Requires administrator privileges. Windows only.

options.name: --uninstall-service
options.description: Uninstalls the smtp4dev Windows service and exits.

options.name: --urls
options.description:
The URLs the web interface should listen on. For
example, http://localhost:123. Use `*` in place
of hostname to listen for requests on any IP
address or hostname using the specified port and
protocol (for example, http://*:5000). Separate
multiple values with ;. For info about HTTPS see
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/
fundamentals/servers/kestrel/endpoints?view=
aspnetcore-8.0#configure-https-in-appsettingsjson

options.name: --basepath
options.description:
Specifies the virtual path from web server root
where SMTP4DEV web interface will be hosted. e.g.
"/" or "/smtp4dev"
